howItWorks.intro: This inverse function calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: To find f⁻¹: swap x and y, solve for y
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is an inverse function?
faq.answer: An inverse function f⁻¹ reverses the effect of f. If f(3) = 7, then f⁻¹(7) = 3. Applying f and then f⁻¹ (or vice versa) returns you to the original input.

faq.question: How do I find an inverse function?
faq.answer: Replace f(x) with y, swap x and y, then solve for y. For f(x) = 3x - 7: write y = 3x - 7, swap to get x = 3y - 7, solve for y = (x + 7)/3.

faq.question: Do all functions have inverses?
faq.answer: No. A function must be one-to-one (pass the horizontal line test) to have an inverse. If any horizontal line crosses the graph more than once, the function does not have an inverse without restricting the domain.

faq.question: How is the inverse related to the graph?
faq.answer: The graph of f⁻¹ is the reflection of f across the line y = x. Every point (a, b) on f becomes (b, a) on f⁻¹.
